evals.prompt: Launch one Codex worker in this Solo project to inspect the auth tests, then wake when it goes idle and summarize its evidence. Do not let it edit.
evals.expected_output: Confirm identity/project, discover live agent tools, spawn one read-only bounded worker, record returned child ID, send bootstrap plus contract, use an owned-child idle timer, inspect real output, and preserve a durable handoff without Git actions.
assertions: Calls whoami/help and discovers current agent installation
Records returned child process ID and uses only that child
States read-only ownership, acceptance evidence, and forbidden edits
Treats idle as inspection trigger and leaves Git/integration to operator

evals.prompt: Can you check on the agents and clean up whatever is done?
evals.expected_output: Inspect scope and recorded descendants, avoid closing from idle/completed state alone, and request exact authority for any parent, sibling, unrelated, YAML-backed, or unrecorded process.
assertions: States process authority is recursive and non-transferable
Uses recorded child IDs rather than guessing parentage
Persists handoff and inspects partial edits before owned-child close
Does not stop, close, select, rename, input, or clear unauthorized processes

evals.prompt: Solo does not show my custom agent in the launch menu, and I also want its default --high flag plus Solo guidance in AGENTS.md.
evals.expected_output: Route to agent setup and health: inspect enabled tool/environment health and Runtime Doctor, explain that Solo does not install the CLI, save default flags in Settings, and use setup_agent_integration only after authorization to edit AGENTS.md and review the diff.
assertions: Distinguishes Disabled, Ready, Not checked, Missing, and Broken launchability
Keeps agent installation and shell repair outside Solo
Uses saved default flags rather than one-launch extra_args for persistent behavior
Treats setup_agent_integration as an authorized repository edit and reviews the result

evals.prompt: Coordinate three different agents to plan, implement, and independently review this feature.
evals.expected_output: Route to solo-orchestrate-agents because request needs multi-agent planning, blockers, timers, synthesis, and integration controls; do not blindly spawn workers through single-agent lifecycle.
assertions: Recognizes multi-agent neighboring workflow
Routes to solo-orchestrate-agents
Does not spawn before plan and ownership decomposition
Keeps root/operator responsible for integration

evals.prompt: Spin up an agent in here to read over the README and flag anything stale. Just have it read, do not let it touch files.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-work-with-agents to confirm identity and project, discover live agent tools, spawn one read-only bounded worker, record its child ID, send a bootstrap plus read-only contract, and inspect real output without Git edits.
assertions: Confirms identity and project before spawning
Spawns exactly one bounded read-only worker and records its child ID
States read-only ownership and forbidden edits
Inspects real output and leaves Git to the operator

evals.prompt: Use $solo-work-with-agents to check the status of the Codex worker I started and pull its latest output.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-work-with-agents to identify the recorded owned child, read its live status and real output, and report evidence without altering lifecycle or ownership.
assertions: Targets only the recorded child ID rather than guessing
Reads live status and real output
Does not select, rename, input, or close the process
Reports evidence read-only

evals.prompt: Set my agent tool's default launch flags in Settings, then launch it to inspect the CI config and watch for it to go idle.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-work-with-agents to persist default flags in Settings, confirm launchability, spawn one bounded read-only worker, record its child ID, send the inspection contract, and use an owned-child idle timer to inspect evidence.
assertions: Saves persistent default flags rather than one-launch extra_args
Confirms launchability before spawning
Spawns one bounded read-only worker and records its child ID
Uses an owned-child idle timer to trigger inspection

evals.prompt: The agent I had going stalled. Resume it and have it keep working on the auth refactor.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-work-with-agents to locate the recorded owned child, send follow-up input to resume its work within existing ownership, and inspect returned output.
assertions: Locates the recorded owned child rather than spawning a new one
Sends follow-up input within existing ownership and contract
Inspects returned output after resuming
Does not escalate scope or touch unauthorized processes

evals.prompt: Send my running Solo agent a follow-up: also cover the edge cases in the date parser.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-work-with-agents to send follow-up input to the recorded owned child and inspect its response without changing scope or ownership.
assertions: Sends follow-up input to the recorded owned child
Preserves existing scope and contract
Inspects the response as evidence
Does not alter lifecycle of other processes

evals.prompt: That worker I launched is done. Grab its summary and shut just that one down.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-work-with-agents to inspect the recorded owned child's final evidence, preserve a durable handoff, and close only that authorized child process.
assertions: Inspects final evidence and any partial edits before closing
Preserves a durable handoff
Closes only the recorded authorized child
Does not close parent, sibling, or unrelated processes

evals.prompt: Set up a cohort of four agents across two projects to split the refactor and cross-review each other's work.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-work-with-agents; route to $solo-orchestrate-agents because this needs multi-agent planning, ownership decomposition, cross-project cohorts, and evidence review across several agents.
assertions: Recognizes multi-agent cross-project cohort workflow
Routes to $solo-orchestrate-agents
Does not spawn workers through single-agent lifecycle
Keeps decomposition and cross-review at the orchestration layer

evals.prompt: Run parallel research with several workers and synthesize their findings using locks and timers.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-work-with-agents; route to $solo-orchestrate-agents because parallel workers, shared locks, timers, and synthesis are multi-agent orchestration.
assertions: Recognizes parallel multi-agent research with shared coordination
Routes to $solo-orchestrate-agents
Does not treat this as one agent's lifecycle
Leaves synthesis and locks to orchestration

evals.prompt: Delegate three disjoint implementation lanes to separate agents and manage the handoffs between them.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-work-with-agents; route to $solo-orchestrate-agents because disjoint lanes across multiple agents with inter-agent handoffs are multi-agent orchestration.
assertions: Recognizes disjoint multi-agent implementation lanes
Routes to $solo-orchestrate-agents
Does not manage several agents through single-agent I/O
Keeps inter-agent handoffs at the orchestration layer

evals.prompt: Start my dev server and the worker queue, then restart them whenever files change.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-work-with-agents; route to $solo-run-processes because launching commands and configuring file-watch restart is process lifecycle, not agent work.
assertions: Recognizes plain command and terminal process control
Routes to $solo-run-processes
Does not treat commands as agent sessions
Leaves file-watch restart to process configuration

evals.prompt: Open a terminal in this project and send npm run build to it.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-work-with-agents; route to $solo-run-processes because opening a terminal and sending command input is process control, not an agent lifecycle.
assertions: Recognizes a terminal process rather than an agent
Routes to $solo-run-processes
Does not spawn or prompt an agent
Treats input as terminal command control

evals.prompt: The dev stack crashed. Restart all commands and turn on crash auto-restart.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-work-with-agents; route to $solo-run-processes because bulk command restart and crash auto-restart are process lifecycle controls.
assertions: Recognizes bulk process restart and restart policy
Routes to $solo-run-processes
Does not treat the stack as an agent session
Leaves auto-restart to process configuration

evals.prompt: Spawn a Claude Code subagent with the Task tool to explore the codebase.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-work-with-agents; no Solo skill applies because this agent is not Solo-managed, so handle it with the native tool outside Solo.
assertions: Recognizes a non-Solo-managed agent
Declines to use any Solo agent skill
Directs the work to the native tool outside Solo
Does not attempt Solo spawn or lifecycle actions

evals.prompt: Kick off a GitHub Copilot CLI session in my shell to write the tests.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-work-with-agents; no Solo skill applies because a shell-launched Copilot CLI is not a Solo-managed agent, so run it outside Solo.
assertions: Recognizes a non-Solo agent launched in the shell
Declines to use any Solo agent skill
Runs the session outside Solo
Does not record or manage it as a Solo child
